h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to simply identify a missed checklist item to authorize the prototype's release.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete release values to teaching the formal documentation of a hold decision when data is missing.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Before this request leaves the counter, place operator recovery in one column: evidence supporting release, or stop condition blocking release.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's specific uncertainty about classifying operator recovery by providing a structural framework to resolve it.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Before this request leaves the counter, place operator recovery in one column: evidence supporting release, or stop condition blocking release.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's specific uncertainty about classifying operator recovery by providing a structural framework to resolve it.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete release values to teaching the formal documentation of a hold decision when data is missing.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Record it in release language, not apology language: checklist complete; release held because hallway-turn contact risk is not bounded by measured clearance, trial speed, or operator control limit.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from demanding concrete release values to teaching the formal documentation of a hold decision when data is missing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say “warned” because it feels vivid
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was attempting to find a compromise phrasing that would allow them to use the vivid verb 'warned' by satisfying the contextual requirements.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: If all four boxes are marked, “shifted toward warning” can stand. If one box is blank, print “repeated under pressure” and let the room do the work.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's lingering attachment to 'warned' by inventing a concrete four-box sign-off mechanism to test the verb's evidentiary warrant.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to demonstrate contingent uptake.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to say “warned” because it feels vivid

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to demonstrate contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: If all four boxes are marked, “shifted toward warning” can stand. If one box is blank, print “repeated under pressure” and let the room do the work.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's lingering attachment to 'warned' by inventing a concrete four-box sign-off mechanism to test the verb's evidentiary warrant.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: If all four boxes are marked, “shifted toward warning” can stand. If one box is blank, print “repeated under pressure” and let the room do the work.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's lingering attachment to 'warned' by inventing a concrete four-box sign-off mechanism to test the verb's evidentiary warrant.

scored.recoheredEarlier: until the limits or other options are actually on the counter
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to evaluate the four paper slips and select the least unfair rationing rule to analyze as instructed.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: “RULE NOT POSTED = PRICE IS THE RULE.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to the learner's wariness of the 'reporting' frame, the tutor shifts to a concrete object-based representation and a new aphoristic framing, forcing a comparison of explicit rationing rules to highlight that price itself is an unposted rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: until the limits or other options are actually on the counter

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: “RULE NOT POSTED = PRICE IS THE RULE.”
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to the learner's wariness of the 'reporting' frame, the tutor shifts to a concrete object-based representation and a new aphoristic framing, forcing a comparison of explicit rationing rules to highlight that price itself is an unposted rule.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: “RULE NOT POSTED = PRICE IS THE RULE.”
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to the learner's wariness of the 'reporting' frame, the tutor shifts to a concrete object-based representation and a new aphoristic framing, forcing a comparison of explicit rationing rules to highlight that price itself is an unposted rule.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The square is the step I was skipping
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to merely accept the area multiplier as a memorized technicality or arithmetic fact.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That last line is where the diagram stops being decoration and becomes proof.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the representational register from physical acetate manipulation to algebraic notation to force the learner to confront the squaring operation structurally.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The square is not acting on a loose 2; it is acting on the whole new side length in both directions.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly builds on the learner's realization about skipping the square by clarifying exactly what the exponent acts upon before generalizing to a variable.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The square is not acting on a loose 2; it is acting on the whole new side length in both directions.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly builds on the learner's realization about skipping the square by clarifying exactly what the exponent acts upon before generalizing to a variable.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: That last line is where the diagram stops being decoration and becomes proof.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the representational register from physical acetate manipulation to algebraic notation to force the learner to confront the squaring operation structurally.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: That last line is where the diagram stops being decoration and becomes proof.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts the representational register from physical acetate manipulation to algebraic notation to force the learner to confront the squaring operation structurally.
